events.title: Wall Street crash — Great Depression begins
events.desc: The October 1929 crash triggers a worldwide depression; US GDP per capita falls by roughly a third by 1933.

events.title: Singapore becomes independent
events.desc: Separated from Malaysia, Singapore starts its rise from low-income port to one of the richest economies per capita.

events.title: OPEC oil embargo — first oil shock
events.desc: Oil prices quadruple within months; oil-exporter incomes spike while the world economy slides into recession.

events.title: UK goes to the IMF
events.desc: The sterling crisis forces Britain to take a $3.9B IMF loan — then the largest ever — in exchange for deep spending cuts.

events.title: China launches Reform and Opening
events.desc: Deng Xiaoping's market reforms begin — the start of the fastest sustained growth episode in recorded history.

events.title: German reunification
events.desc: FRG and GDR unite; trillion-euro transfers between west and east reshape German income data for years.

events.title: Japan's asset bubble bursts
events.desc: Stock and land prices collapse after the BOJ tightens; the 'Lost Decades' of stagnation begin.

events.title: India liberalizes its economy
events.desc: A balance-of-payments crisis forces the Rao–Singh reforms: licenses scrapped, trade opened — growth accelerates durably.

events.title: Plano Real ends hyperinflation
events.desc: Brazil's new currency, the real, breaks inflation running above 2,000% a year and stabilizes the economy.

events.title: Asian financial crisis hits Korea
events.desc: Capital flight forces South Korea into a $58B IMF package — then the largest ever — followed by rapid reform and recovery.

events.title: Russia defaults on its debt
events.desc: Moscow defaults on domestic bonds and devalues the ruble; LTCM collapses in the global fallout.

events.title: China joins the WTO
events.desc: Accession to the World Trade Organization supercharges export-led growth; China becomes the workshop of the world.

events.title: Global financial crisis
events.desc: Lehman Brothers fails; the Great Recession ripples through incomes worldwide for years.

events.title: CCB goes public in Hong Kong
events.desc: China Construction Bank raises $9.2B — the first of China's big-four state banks to list.

events.title: MUFG is formed
events.desc: Mitsubishi Tokyo Financial Group and UFJ Holdings merge, creating one of the world's largest banks.

events.title: ICBC's record-breaking IPO
events.desc: Industrial & Commercial Bank of China raises $21.9B — the largest IPO in history at the time.

events.title: JPMorgan absorbs Bear Stearns and WaMu
events.desc: In the depths of the crisis JPMorgan takes over Bear Stearns ($2/share, Fed-backed) and Washington Mutual's assets.

events.title: BofA buys Merrill Lynch
events.desc: Bank of America acquires Merrill Lynch for ~$50B at the height of the panic — later needing $45B in TARP aid.

events.title: Citigroup bailout
events.desc: Citi receives $45B in TARP capital plus guarantees on $306B of assets to avert collapse.

events.title: Fannie Mae in conservatorship
events.desc: The US government places Fannie Mae under conservatorship and backstops it with up to $100B.

events.title: Freddie Mac in conservatorship
events.desc: Freddie Mac joins Fannie Mae under government control — the mortgage giants are nationalized in all but name.

events.title: AgBank's world-record IPO
events.desc: Agricultural Bank of China raises $22.1B, overtaking ICBC as the largest IPO ever (a record it held until Alibaba 2014).

events.title: HSBC's $1.9B laundering settlement
events.desc: HSBC pays a record US fine for laundering drug-cartel and sanctioned-country money.

events.title: BNP Paribas fined $8.9B
events.desc: BNP pleads guilty to US sanctions violations — the largest penalty ever imposed on a bank at that point.

events.title: Japan Post privatization IPO
events.desc: Japan Post Holdings lists in a ¥1.4 trillion offering — the world's biggest IPO of 2015.

events.title: Fake-accounts scandal
events.desc: Wells Fargo admits staff opened millions of unauthorized accounts; $185M in fines, the CEO resigns, the brand is damaged for years.

events.title: First Walmart opens
events.desc: Sam Walton opens the first Walmart in Rogers, Arkansas — the foundation of the Walton family's retail fortune.

events.title: Buffett takes over Berkshire Hathaway
events.desc: Warren Buffett assumes control of a struggling textile mill and turns it into his investment holding company.

events.title: Microsoft founded
events.desc: Bill Gates and Paul Allen start Microsoft in Albuquerque to sell a BASIC interpreter.

events.title: First Zara store
events.desc: Amancio Ortega opens the first Zara shop in A Coruña — the seed of the Inditex fast-fashion empire.

events.title: Oracle founded
events.desc: Larry Ellison co-founds Software Development Laboratories — later renamed Oracle after its database.

events.title: Bloomberg LP founded
events.desc: After leaving Salomon Brothers, Michael Bloomberg builds the terminal business that funds his fortune.

events.title: Arnault wins control of LVMH
events.desc: Bernard Arnault prevails in the battle for LVMH and builds it into the world's largest luxury group.

events.title: NVIDIA founded
events.desc: Jensen Huang co-founds NVIDIA — three decades later its GPUs power the AI boom and make him one of the richest people alive.

events.title: Amazon founded
events.desc: Jeff Bezos quits D.E. Shaw and starts Amazon as an online bookstore from a Seattle garage.

events.title: Google founded
events.desc: Larry Page and Sergey Brin incorporate Google in a Menlo Park garage.

events.title: Musk starts SpaceX
events.desc: Elon Musk invests ~$100M of his PayPal proceeds into SpaceX; a year later he joins Tesla as lead investor.

events.title: Google IPO
events.desc: Google goes public at $85/share via its unconventional Dutch auction, making Brin and Page billionaires.

events.title: Facebook founded in a dorm
events.desc: Mark Zuckerberg launches TheFacebook from his Harvard room; within a decade it reaches a billion users.

events.title: Reliance Jio launches
events.desc: Mukesh Ambani's Jio floods India with free 4G data, upending the telecom market and re-rating Reliance.
